sections.heading: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection
sections.summary: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection (Complaints Handling Procedure and Enforcement) Regulations, 2021.
sections.source_quote: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection (Complaints Handling Procedure and Enforcement) Regulations, 2021.

sections.heading: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires
sections.summary: Defines terms including data subject, Data Protection Commissioner, and related concepts.

requirements.text: means a data subject or a person who has lodged a complaint pursuant to regulation 4
means the person appointed under section 6 of the Act
means the office of the Data Protection Commissioner
Commissioner under regulation 16
Commissioner under regulation 20
lodged
writing, directing a person to appear before the Office

sections.source_quote: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires means a data subject or a person who has lodged a complaint pursuant to regulation 4; means the person appointed under section 6 of the Act; means the office of the Data Protection Commissioner; Commissioner under regulation 16; Commissioner under regulation 20; lodged; and writing, directing a person to appear before the Office.

sections.heading: The object and purpose of these Regulations is to
sections.summary: Regulations aim to facilitate fair complaint determination, issue enforcement notices, penalty notices, and provide procedures for complaint resolution.
sections.source_quote: The object and purpose of these Regulations is to facilitate a fair, impartial, just, expeditious, proportionate and affordable determination of complaints lodged with the Data Commissioner in accordance with the Act and these Regulations, without undue regard to technicalities of procedure; provide for issuance of enforcement notices as contemplated under section 58 of the Act; provide for issuance of penalty notices as contemplated under section 62 of the Act; provide for the procedure for hearing and determining of complaints; and provide for the resolution of complaints lodged with the Data Commissioner by means of alternative dispute resolution mechanisms as specified under section 9(1)(c) of the Act.

sections.heading: (1) Pursuant to section 56 of the Act, a data subject or any
sections.summary: Complaints may be lodged orally, electronically, or by other means; free of charge; acknowledged within seven days.
sections.dtia_summary: Complaints must be lodged free of charge and acknowledged within seven days.

requirements.text: A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged orally, subject to section 56(3) of the Act
A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged through electronic means, including email, web posting, complaint management information system
A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged by the complainant in person
A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged by a person acting on behalf of the complainant
A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged by any other person authorized by law to act on behalf of a data subject
A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged anonymously
The Data Commissioner shall acknowledge receipt of the complaint within seven days of receipt of the complaint under sub-regulation (1)
A complaint under sub-regulation (1) shall be lodged free of charge

sections.source_quote: (1) Pursuant to section 56 of the Act, a data subject or any person aggrieved on any matter under the Act may lodge a complaint with the Data Commissioner. (2) A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged in Form DPC 1 set out in the Schedule (a) orally, subject to section 56(3) of the Act; (b) through electronic means, including email, web posting, complaint management information system; or (c) by any other appropriate means. (4) The Data Commissioner shall acknowledge receipt of the complaint within seven days of receipt of the complaint under sub-regulation (1). (5) The complaint under sub-regulation (1) shall be lodged free of charge.

sections.heading: (1) The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to
sections.summary: Data Commissioner maintains a Register of Complaints with complainant details and may protect complainant identity.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Commissioner must maintain a Register of Complaints with complainant details and protect identity where requested.

requirements.text: The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date Register of Complaints
An entry into the register of complaints shall state the particulars of the complainant and the complaint filed with the Data Commissioner
The Data Commissioner shall protect the identity of the complainant where the request to protect the identity is sought by the complainant

sections.source_quote: (1) The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date Register of Complaints. (2) An entry into the register of complaints shall state the particulars of the complainant and the complaint filed with the Data Commissioner. (3) The Data Commissioner shall protect the identity of the complainant where the request to protect the identity is sought by the complainant.

sections.heading: (1) The Data Commissioner shall undertake a preliminary
sections.summary: Data Commissioner undertakes preliminary review of complaints; may admit, refer, or decline complaints; may conduct inquiries or mediation.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Commissioner must preliminarily review complaints; may admit, refer, or decline complaints; may conduct inquiries or mediation.

requirements.text: The Data Commissioner shall undertake a preliminary review of a complaint, upon receipt of the complaint by the Office

The Data Commissioner may, upon undertaking a preliminary review of the complaint (a) admit the complaint; (b) where applicable, advise the complainant in writing that the matter is not within the mandate of the Data Commissioner; or (c) advise the complainant that the matter lies for determination by another body or institution and refer the complainant to that body or institution
Despite sub-regulation (2), the Data Commissioner may decline to admit a complaint where the complaint does not raise any issue under the Act

Upon admission of a complaint, the Data Commissioner may (a) conduct an inquiry into the complaint; (b) conduct investigations; (c) facilitate mediation, conciliation or negotiation in accordance with the Act and these Regulations; or (d) use any other mechanisms to resolve the complaint

Where a complaint is declined for admission under sub-regulation (3), the complaint may be re-admitted within six months from the date of decline, where the complaint raises new issues for determination under the Act

sections.source_quote: (1) The Data Commissioner shall undertake a preliminary review of a complaint, upon receipt of the complaint by the Office. (2) The Data Commissioner may, upon undertaking a preliminary review of the complaint (a) admit the complaint; (b) where applicable, advise the complainant in writing that the matter is not within the mandate of the Data Commissioner; or (c) advise the complainant that the matter lies for determination by another body or institution and refer the complainant to that body or institution. (3) Despite sub-regulation (2), the Data Commissioner may decline to admit a complaint where the complaint does not raise any issue under the Act. (4) Upon admission of a complaint, the Data Commissioner may (a) conduct an inquiry into the complaint; (b) conduct investigations; (c) facilitate mediation, conciliation or negotiation in accordance with the Act and these Regulations; or (d) use any other mechanisms to resolve the complaint. (5) Where a complaint is declined for admission under sub-regulation (3), the complaint may be re-admitted within six months from the date of decline, where the complaint raises new issues for determination under the Act.

sections.heading: (1) The Data Commissioner may discontinue an existing
sections.summary: Data Commissioner may discontinue complaints where they do not merit further consideration or complainant fails to communicate.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Commissioner may discontinue complaints where they do not merit further consideration or complainant fails to communicate; must notify reasons within 14 days.

requirements.text: The Data Commissioner may discontinue an existing complaint in Form DPC 2 set out in the Schedule, where (a) a complaint does not merit further consideration; or (b) a complainant refuses, fails or neglects to communicate without justifiable cause

The Data Commissioner shall provide the reasons for discontinuation on any of the grounds specified under sub-regulation (1) (a) or (b) and shall, in writing, notify the complainant and respondent within fourteen days from the date the decision to discontinue a complaint is made
A complainant may, where a complaint has been discontinued pursuant to these Regulations, re-institute a complaint upon providing grounds for the restitution to the Data Commissioner

sections.source_quote: (1) The Data Commissioner may discontinue an existing complaint in Form DPC 2 set out in the Schedule, where (a) a complaint does not merit further consideration; or (b) a complainant refuses, fails or neglects to communicate without justifiable cause. (2) The Data Commissioner shall provide the reasons for discontinuation on any of the grounds specified under sub-regulation (1) (a) or (b) and shall, in writing, notify the complainant and respondent within fourteen days from the date the decision to discontinue a complaint is made. (3) A complainant may, where a complaint has been discontinued pursuant to these Regulations, re-institute a complaint upon providing grounds for the restitution to the Data Commissioner.

sections.heading: (1) A complaint may be withdrawn at any stage during its
sections.summary: Complaints may be withdrawn at any stage before determination; re-lodged within six months.
sections.dtia_summary: Complaints may be withdrawn at any stage before determination; re-lodged within six months.

requirements.text: A complaint may be withdrawn at any stage during its consideration but before a determination is made
A complainant may, at any time during the consideration of a complaint lodged pursuant to regulation 4 and before its determination, withdraw the complaint
A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged in Form DPC  1 set out in the Schedule
A complainant may re-institute a complaint upon providing grounds for the restitution to the Data Commissioner

sections.source_quote: (1) A complaint may be withdrawn at any stage during its consideration but before a determination is made. (2) A complainant may, at any time during the consideration of a complaint lodged pursuant to regulation 4 and before its determination, withdraw the complaint. (3) A complaint lodged under sub-regulation (1) may be lodged in Form DPC 1 set out in the Schedule.

sections.heading: (1) Where two or more complaints are lodged in which similar
sections.summary: Complaints may be resolved through negotiation, mediation, or conciliation; otherwise, determined by the Data Commissioner.
sections.dtia_summary: Complaints may be resolved through negotiation, mediation, or conciliation; otherwise, determined by the Data Commissioner.

requirements.text: Parties to a dispute shall take all reasonable measures to amicably determine a dispute and act in good faith
Where the complaint is not determined through negotiation, mediation or conciliation, the Data Commissioner shall proceed to determine the complaint as provided for in the Act and these Regulations

sections.source_quote: Parties to a dispute shall take all reasonable measures to amicably determine a dispute and act in good faith. Where the complaint is not determined through negotiation, mediation or conciliation, the Data Commissioner shall proceed to determine the complaint as provided for in the Act and these Regulations.

sections.heading: (1) Proceedings before the Office shall be conducted in
sections.summary: Data Commissioner may issue enforcement notices under the Regulations or section 58 of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Commissioner may issue enforcement notices under the Regulations or section 58 of the Act.

requirements.text: The Data Commissioner may pursuant these Regulations or section 58 of the Act issue an enforcement notice

sections.source_quote: The Data Commissioner may pursuant these Regulations or section 58 of the Act issue an enforcement notice.

sections.heading: (1) Upon admission of a complaint, the Data Commissioner
sections.summary: Enforcement notices are deemed served via electronic delivery or physical delivery to registered offices.
sections.dtia_summary: Enforcement notices are deemed served via electronic delivery or physical delivery to registered offices.

requirements.text: An enforcement notice shall be deemed to have been duly served on the concerned person where (a) an electronic copy of enforcement notice is sent to the (b) the enforcement notice is posted or physically delivered to the registered offices of the concerned person, in the absence of an electronic address

sections.source_quote: An enforcement notice shall be deemed to have been duly served on the concerned person where (a) an electronic copy of enforcement notice is sent to the (b) the enforcement notice is posted or physically delivered to the registered offices of the concerned person, in the absence of an electronic address.

sections.heading: (1) Where it appears to the Data Commissioner, or by
sections.summary: Persons may apply for review of enforcement notices before the end of the specified period or on grounds of changed circumstances.
sections.dtia_summary: Persons may apply for review of enforcement notices before the end of the specified period or on grounds of changed circumstances.

requirements.text: A person to whom an enforcement notice is given may apply in Form DPC 6 set out in the Schedule to the Data Commissioner for a review of the enforcement notice

An application under sub-regulation (1) may be made (a) before the end of the period specified in the enforcement notice; and (b) on the ground that (i) a change of circumstances or new facts have arisen; or (ii) one or more of the provisions of that notice need not be complied with in order to remedy the failure identified in the notice

sections.source_quote: (1) A person to whom an enforcement notice is given may apply in Form DPC 6 set out in the Schedule to the Data Commissioner for a review of the enforcement notice. (2) An application under sub-regulation (1) may be made (a) before the end of the period specified in the enforcement notice; and (b) on the ground that (i) a change of circumstances or new facts have arisen; or (ii) one or more of the provisions of that notice need not be complied with in order to remedy the failure identified in the notice.

sections.heading: (1) In investigating a complaint, the Data Commissioner
sections.summary: Persons may appeal against enforcement notices to the High Court within thirty days of service.
sections.dtia_summary: Persons may appeal against enforcement notices to the High Court within thirty days of service.

requirements.text: Subject to sections 58 (2) (d) and 64 of the Act, a person may before the lapse of thirty days from the date of service of the enforcement notice, appeal to the High Court against a decision arising out of the enforcement of the notice

sections.source_quote: Subject to sections 58 (2) (d) and 64 of the Act, a person may before the lapse of thirty days from the date of service of the enforcement notice, appeal to the High Court against a decision arising out of the enforcement of the notice.

sections.heading: (1) The Data Commissioner shall, upon the conclusion of the
sections.summary: Data Commissioner issues penalty notices for breaches identified in enforcement notices.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Commissioner issues penalty notices for breaches identified in enforcement notices.

requirements.text: The Data Commissioner shall, where any of the circumstances specified under section 62 of the Act and these Regulations arises, issue a penalty notice for each breach identified in the enforcement notice

A penalty notice shall contain (a) the name and address of the concerned person, to whom it is addressed; (b) the reasons why the Data Commissioner proposes to impose the penalty and the amount thereof; (c) an administrative fine imposed as contemplated under section 63 of the Act; (d) details of how the penalty is to be paid; and (e) details of the rights of appeal under section 64 of the Act
The administrative fine levied under sub-regulation (2)(c) shall consider each individual case and have due regard to factors or reasons outlined under section 62 (2) of the Act
A penalty notice may impose a daily fine of not more than ten thousand shillings for each breach identified until the breach is rectified

sections.source_quote: (1) The Data Commissioner shall, where any of the circumstances specified under section 62 of the Act and these Regulations arises, issue a penalty notice for each breach identified in the enforcement notice. (2) A penalty notice shall contain (a) the name and address of the concerned person, to whom it is addressed; (b) the reasons why the Data Commissioner proposes to impose the penalty and the amount thereof; (c) an administrative fine imposed as contemplated under section 63 of the Act; (d) details of how the penalty is to be paid; and (e) details of the rights of appeal under section 64 of the Act. (3) The administrative fine levied under sub-regulation (2)(c) shall consider each individual case and have due regard to factors or reasons outlined under section 62 (2) of the Act. (4) A penalty notice may impose a daily fine of not more than ten thousand shillings for each breach identified until the breach is rectified.

(1) The Data Commissioner shall, where any of the
The Data Commissioner shall enforce or take action to
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner may enforce penalties by recovering penalties after specified periods or upon final appeal determination.
sections.dtia_summary: Penalty enforcement timelines and appeal mechanisms are critical for DTIA compliance.

requirements.text: recover a penalty upon the lapse of the period specified in the penalty notice for payment of the penalty
recover a penalty on the final determination of any appeal against the penalty notice
recover a penalty on the lapse of the period given to appeal against the penalty

sections.source_quote: 21. The Data Commissioner shall enforce or take action to recover a penalty (a) upon the lapse of the period specified in the penalty notice for payment of the penalty; (b) on the final determination of any appeal against the penalty notice; or (c) on the lapse of the period given to appeal against the penalty.
